pattern: Simulate the walk directly: only detour back to the river to refill when the can genuinely cannot fully satisfy the very next plant, and add up every stretch of ground actually covered.
hints: Track your current position and how much water remains in the can as you move plant by plant; a refill trip is only ever a round trip back to position -1 and then out again.

Before watering plant i, compare the water left against exactly that plant's requirement — if it falls short, walk back to the river (adding position + 1 steps), refill to full, then walk out to plant i (adding i + 1 steps) before pouring.

If the can already holds enough, just add the distance from wherever you are to plant i and pour; a plant that consumes water down to exactly zero still doesn't trigger a refill until the NEXT plant actually needs more than is left.

edge_inputs.description: four plants with one refill trip triggered partway through the row
six plants where the demanding fourth plant forces the first of two refills
capacity equals every plant's need exactly, so a refill happens before every single plant after the first
the can holds exactly enough for the next plant, which must NOT trigger a refill (< not <=)
a single plant whose need equals the capacity exactly, watered straight from the starting full can
capacity is far beyond any single need, so the can never runs low after the first fill
capacity matches the largest requirement, but only some plants actually need that much
alternating heavy and light requirements forcing a refill before every heavy plant

stress.description: random plant rows up to the length cap with a random capacity at least as large as the heaviest plant
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 1000)))
    plants = [rng.randint(1, 1000000) for _ in range(n)]
    capacity = rng.randint(max(plants), 1000000000)
    return [plants, capacity]
